report_name: Tropic Trooper APT Targets Chinese Speakers with Trojanized PDF Reader, Uses GitHub for C2

threat_actors.name: Tropic Trooper
threat_actors.description: A cyber-espionage group that targeted Chinese-speaking individuals in Taiwan, South Korea, and Japan in March 2026. The campaign used a trojanized SumatraPDF reader to deploy the AdaptixC2 Beacon, which leverages GitHub for C2 and VS Code tunnels for persistence.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: The victim was socially engineered to execute a trojanized SumatraPDF reader disguised as a military-themed document within a ZIP archive.

attack_patterns.name: Deobfuscate/Decode Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The TOSHIS loader downloaded an encrypted shellcode from a staging server, which was then decrypted and executed in memory to launch the AdaptixC2 Beacon.

attack_patterns.name: Web Service: Bidirectional Communication
attack_patterns.description: The AdaptixC2 Beacon used the GitHub API for command and control, reading commands from GitHub issues and exfiltrating data by posting to a repository.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The TOSHIS loader connected to a staging server to download the next-stage encrypted shellcode. Additionally, Microsoft Visual Studio Code was deployed to high-value targets for persistence.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Access Software
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors used Microsoft Visual Studio Code tunnels to establish persistent, encrypted remote access to high-value targets, blending in with legitimate developer traffic.

malware.name: AdaptixC2 Beacon
malware.description: A custom post-exploitation agent deployed by the TOSHIS loader. It uses the GitHub API for command and control by reading commands from issues and exfiltrating data to a repository.

malware.name: TOSHIS
malware.description: A loader, identified as a variant of Xiangoop, that is executed by the trojanized SumatraPDF. It connects to a staging server to download and execute an encrypted shellcode payload, which in turn launches the AdaptixC2 Beacon.

malware.name: EntryShell
malware.description: A custom backdoor previously associated with Tropic Trooper, found hosted on the campaign's staging server.

tools.name: SumatraPDF
tools.description: A legitimate PDF reader that was trojanized and used as a lure. Upon execution, it displayed a decoy document while deploying the TOSHIS loader in the background.

tools.name: Cobalt Strike
tools.description: A Cobalt Strike Beacon was found hosted on the staging server, indicating its use as part of the attacker's toolset for post-exploitation activities.

tools.name: Microsoft Visual Studio Code
tools.description: The legitimate developer tool was abused by the threat actors to establish persistent, encrypted remote access via VS Code tunnels for high-value targets.

identities.name: Chinese-speaking individuals and organizations
identities.description: The primary target demographic of the Tropic Trooper campaign, located in Taiwan, South Korea, and Japan, with a likely focus on government, defense, or political sectors based on military-themed lures.

infrastructure.name: Tropic Trooper Staging Server
infrastructure.description: An IP address (158.247.193.100) used to host and deliver payloads, including an encrypted shellcode for the AdaptixC2 beacon, a Cobalt Strike Beacon, and the EntryShell backdoor.

infrastructure.name: GitHub C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The threat actors used GitHub repositories and its API for command and control of the AdaptixC2 Beacon. Commands were read from GitHub issues and exfiltrated data was posted to the repository.

infrastructure.name: VS Code Tunnel Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Microsoft Visual Studio Code tunnels were used to provide persistent, encrypted remote access to high-value targets, blending C2 traffic with legitimate developer activity.

observables.name: Comparative Analysis of US-UK and US-Australia Nuclear Submarine Cooperation (2025).exe

indicators.name: Tropic Trooper Staging Server IP
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Tropic Trooper Staging Server Connection
id: 9b2d8c3f-1e4a-4b7c-8d5e-6f9a0b1c2d3e
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound network connections to a known Tropic Trooper staging server IP address used to host payloads.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/tropic-trooper-uses-trojanized.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-24
tags:
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationIp: '158.247.193.100'
    Initiated: true
  condition: selection

indicators.name: Tropic Trooper Lure Filename Execution
indicators.stix_pattern: [file:name = 'Comparative Analysis of US-UK and US-Australia Nuclear Submarine Cooperation (2025).exe']
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Tropic Trooper Lure File Execution
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of a malicious file used as a lure in a Tropic Trooper campaign.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/tropic-trooper-uses-trojanized.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-24
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\Comparative Analysis of US-UK and US-Australia Nuclear Submarine Cooperation (2025).exe'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: Tropic Trooper GitHub C2 Pattern
indicators.pattern:
title: Tropic Trooper C2 Communication via GitHub API
id: f8a1b2c3-d4e5-4f6a-7b8c-9d0e1f2a3b4c
status: experimental
description: Detects network traffic patterns consistent with the AdaptixC2 beacon using GitHub issues for command and control.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/tropic-trooper-uses-trojanized.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-24
tags:
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains|all:
      - 'api.github.com/repos/'
      - '/issues'
  filter:
    c-useragent|contains:
      - 'GitHub'
      - 'git/'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to GitHub API endpoints for issues, which is used by AdaptixC2 for C2. This is a behavioral hint and may have false positives from legitimate developer tools.

indicators.name: VS Code Tunnel Command-Line Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Visual Studio Code Tunnel Execution
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the 'code tunnel' command, which can be used by threat actors like Tropic Trooper for persistent remote access.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/tropic-trooper-uses-trojanized.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-24
tags:
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\code.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' tunnel'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the command-line execution used to initiate a VS Code tunnel, a technique used by Tropic Trooper for persistence.

indicators.name: VS Code Tunnel Network Connection
indicators.pattern:
title: Visual Studio Code Tunnel Network Connection
id: e5f6a7b8-c9d0-4e1f-8a2b-3c4d5e6f7a8b
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound network connections to the domain used for VS Code tunnels. Connections from servers or non-developer workstations are highly suspect.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/tropic-trooper-uses-trojanized.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-24
tags:
  - attack.t1219
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith: '.rel.tunnels.api.visualstudio.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries for the domain used by VS Code tunnels. While potentially legitimate, it can indicate malicious persistence when observed from unexpected systems.

relationships.target_name: Comparative Analysis of US-UK and US-Australia Nuclear Submarine Cooperation (2025).exe
